domain: Bambara Bogolanfini mud cloth (Mali Bambara; mud-cloth resist-dye technique; women apply fermented mud onto pre-dyed cotton; pattern emerges as untouched un-dyed areas; chemistry of fermented mud + n'gallama leaf dye)
specific_mechanism: Subtractive LoRA adapter design: ΔW_neg = -α · (v_undesired · v_undesired^T) · W_base, applied along a learned-undesired direction v in the base weight space, with v ∈ null-space of currently-active task directions. Adapter is sign-NEGATIVE by construction — RESISTS/SUBTRACTS specific undesired behaviors (toxicity, bias direction, hallucination-prone direction) rather than adding new capability.
llm_application: BOGOLAN-NEGATIVE: a sign-NEGATIVE LoRA adapter for direction-subtraction. (1) Compute v_undesired = mean activation difference between undesired and desired generations on calibration prompts (e.g., toxic vs safe pairs); (2) project v_undesired onto null-space of in-task signal v_task via Gram-Schmidt to avoid task-degradation; (3) adapter weight is sign-NEGATIVE: ΔW = -α · v · v^T (rank-1 negative outer product); (4) FT trains α and v jointly with KL-regularized loss to preserve base behavior + ablate v direction. Differs from LoRA-Null 2503.02659 (null-space init, adds capability), LoRA Subtraction 2503.18985 (subtraction for drift-resistance, not direction-ablation), Safe LoRA 2405.16833 (projects to safety subspace, not negates direction), OPLoRA 2510.13003 (orthogonal projection prevents forgetting). Differs from R414 KAREN-WEFT-NULL (joint param+activation null) by SIGN-NEGATIVE direction-ablation; R427 ULI-EPHEMERAL (time-decay, not direction-negation).
novelty_claim: LoRA-Null + LoRA Subtraction + Safe LoRA + OPLoRA cover null-space adapter + projection space. BOGOLAN-NEGATIVE adds (a) explicit sign-NEGATIVE rank-1 outer-product adapter + (b) direction-ablation interpretation for undesired-behavior subtraction. Recombination of direction-ablation literature + null-space LoRA.
